title: branch — retention windows
description: Branch rows carry no PII directly (the venue's address is public-adjacent; the phone / email are business contacts, not personal PII). Retention is driven by compliance material — closed branches are referenced by historic invoices, activity logs, and audit trails, so they persist indefinitely. Hard-delete is a two-person platform-support action, never a public API surface.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation — Branch itself is not personal data but audit rows referencing it are)
gdpr Art. 17 (right to erasure — cascades from tenant erasure only)
financial reporting regulations (many jurisdictions require 7-year invoice retention — invoices reference branch_id snapshot)
labor + occupancy regulations (fire-safety records may reference a venue for the venue's lifetime + several years post-closure)

hot.days: indefinite (compliance material — referenced by historic invoices + fire-safety records + audit rows for the venue's full lifetime)

purge.trigger: platform-support two-person approval via `branch:hard-delete --platform-support --ticket=<url>` CLI (never public API)
purge.action: hard delete — CASCADE removes tenant_id-linked rows; RESTRICT on downstream domain FKs means the operator must clean up dependents first (documented in the CLI's runbook)
purge.notes: Only used when a Branch was created in error or via test-tenant setup. Real closures stay in `status=closed` forever.

hot.days: removed atomically with the parent Tenant

purge.action: hard delete via CASCADE — the `BranchesOnTenantErased` hook cancels in-flight geocoding jobs before the DB cascade runs

cold.days: up to 7 years per compliance tier — Small=90d / Medium=1yr / Enterprise=configurable up to 7yr per hierarchy.md §7

purge.notes: Audit rows OUTLIVE their Branch subject. The compliance signal is: 'when was this closure decided, who approved it, what were the tenant conditions at the time.' A hard-deleted Branch still shows up in audits keyed by branch_id ULID.
